q: What does 'radioaktiv' mean in English?
e: Radioaktiv (adjective) means radioactive—emitting radiation. Example: 'Die ganze Region ist radioaktiv verseucht.' (The entire region is radioactively contaminated.)

q: What does 'das Weib' mean in English?
e: Das Weib (noun, neuter) is an archaic and derogatory German word for woman. Modern German prefers 'Frau' (woman/Mrs.). Example: 'Seine Nachbarin ist ein furchtbares Weib.' (His neighbor is a dreadful woman.)

s: 'das Volumen' means 'volume, total amount' in German
q: What does 'das Volumen' mean in English?
e: Das Volumen (noun, neuter) refers to volume or total amount—the three-dimensional space occupied by an object. Example: 'Der Auftrag hat ein Volumen von ca. 50 000 Euro.' (The contract has a total volume of approximately 50,000 euros.)

q: What does 'der Bestandteil' mean in English?
e: Der Bestandteil (noun, masculine) refers to a component or integral part of something. Example: 'Zwei Praktika sind obligatorische Bestandteile dieses Studiums.' (Two internships are mandatory components of this program.)

s: 'vorbeigehen' means 'to go past, pass' in German
q: What does 'vorbeigehen' mean in English?
e: Vorbeigehen (verb) means to go past or pass by—to move past something without stopping. Example: 'Alles geht vorbei.' (Everything passes/goes by.)

q: What does 'übertreiben' mean in English?
e: Übertreiben (verb) means to exaggerate—to represent something as greater than it actually is. Example: 'Jetzt übertreibst du aber!' (Now you're exaggerating!)

q: What does 'letztlich' mean in English?
e: Letztlich (adverb) means in the end or ultimately—used to indicate a final result. Example: 'Letztlich hat er doch zugestimmt.' (In the end, he did agree.)

q: What does 'der Zuhörer' mean in English?
e: Der Zuhörer (noun, masculine) refers to a listener or member of an audience. Example: 'Daniel ist ein guter Zuhörer.' (Daniel is a good listener.)

s: ascorbic acid is needed by the body in small amounts
q: Which English word means 'an essential nutrient that the body needs in small amounts'?
e: Das Vitamin (noun, neuter) refers to a vitamin—an essential organic compound needed for healthy body function. Example: 'Kiwis enthalten viel Vitamin C.' (Kiwis contain lots of vitamin C.)

q: What does 'fangen' mean in English?
e: Fangen (verb) means to catch—to capture or grab something. Example: 'Die Polizei fängt den Einbrecher.' (The police catch the burglar.)

q: What does 'der Zugriff' mean in English?
e: Der Zugriff (noun, masculine) refers to access—the ability or right to use something. Example: 'Auf diese Daten hat nur der Administrator Zugriff.' (Only the administrator has access to this data.)

q: What does 'zweifellos' mean in English?
e: Zweifellos (adjective/adverb) means undoubtedly or without doubt. Example: 'Das bekannteste optische Gerät ist zweifellos die Brille.' (The most well-known optical device is undoubtedly eyeglasses.)

q: What does 'die Ermittlung' mean in English?
e: Die Ermittlung (noun, feminine) refers to an investigation—the process of inquiring into something. Example: 'Die Ermittlungen sind noch nicht abgeschlossen.' (The investigations are not yet complete.)

q: What does 'westdeutsch' mean in English?
e: Westdeutsch (adjective) means West German—referring to the Federal Republic of Germany (1949-1990). Example: 'Westdeutsche Produkte konnte man manchmal auch in der DDR kaufen.' (West German products could sometimes be bought in East Germany.)

q: What does 'vortragen' mean in English?
e: Vortragen (verb) means to present or perform something publicly. Example: 'Die Projektgruppe trägt ihre Ergebnisse vor.' (The project group presents their results.)
